summary: In this brief interview clip, Elizabeth Anscombe reflects on Ludwig Wittgenstein's philosophical approach and personal qualities. She highlights his unique ability to understand the root causes of people's philosophical beliefs, which allowed him to offer profound rather than superficial refutations, and praises his tailored judgment in recommending reading material.

principles.statement: Effective philosophical refutation requires understanding exactly why someone holds their belief.
principles.rationale: Without understanding the root cause or 'nerve' of a person's thought process, any attempt to undermine their argument will be merely superficial.
principles.source_quote: he had an extraordinary understanding of why people thought the things that they did think in philosophical argument so that when he undermined it his undermining showed that he was getting at the the nerve the root it was not a superficial refutation

signature_quotes.text: he had an extraordinary understanding of why people thought the things that they did think in philosophical argument
signature_quotes.context: Discussing Ludwig Wittgenstein's approach to understanding others.

signature_quotes.text: when he undermined it his undermining showed that he was getting at the the nerve the root it was not a superficial refutation
signature_quotes.context: Describing how Wittgenstein dismantled philosophical arguments.

anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to address the 'nerve' or 'root' of why the person actually believes what they do.
